summary: v2.6d Admin Mini-Dashboard — full backend + frontend testing PASSED. Backend: 24/24 new pytest in test_admin_v26d.py + full regression 130/130 (106 previous + 24 new). Frontend: PIN gate (wrong/correct/localStorage cache/logout), 6 stat tiles, search (case-insensitive, by name/nopol/order_id), status filter, full status workflow (NEW→DISPATCHED→ON_TRIP→DELIVERED), convert modal with prefilled defaults & toast 'Trip dibuat: TRIP-...', inline driver assign with mirror to linked trip, cancel button removed after delivery, mobile responsive (390px viewport 2-col stat grid). Navy+Gold theme clean, zero console errors.

minor.issue: Invalid status value is silently ignored (no filter applied) rather than returning 400. Per spec, status enum validation is documented for PATCH only, so this is acceptable but worth documenting.

integration_issues.issue: Convert writes driver_id to the new trip but NOT back to the order document. Result: after convert, order.driver_id stays null while trip.driver_id holds the value. Admin must then use inline edit (PATCH /admin/orders) to populate order.driver_id, which DOES mirror correctly to the trip. Minor data-consistency nit — UX still works because the Driver row reads from order.driver_id and shows 'belum di-assign' until admin clicks ✎.

issues: Navy + Gold theme renders crisply on both desktop (1366px) and mobile (390px). Stat tiles wrap to 2-col on mobile as intended. Active stat tile highlight (Baru orange ring) clearly visible. Modal overlay + form inputs aligned. No contrast or alignment issues observed.

action_items: (MINOR) Consider mirroring driver_id from convert payload to BOTH order and trip documents in POST /api/orders/{id}/convert (currently only writes to trip). Today admin must inline-edit driver after convert to populate order.driver_id. Non-blocking; PATCH flow already mirrors correctly.
(NIT) GET /api/admin/orders silently drops invalid status values — could return 400 for consistency with PATCH validation, but current behaviour is forgiving and probably preferable for URL fiddling.
critical_code_review_comments: require_admin_pin (server.py:38-45) is clean: header-based, 503 when env unset, 401 on mismatch. Dependency injected via Depends() on all 3 admin endpoints — properly guarded.
/admin/auth (line 856-864) reuses the same env-lookup pattern instead of calling require_admin_pin (because PIN comes in body, not header). Acceptable duplication; logic is identical.
/admin/stats uses single Mongo aggregation pipeline + one count_documents — efficient. Returns all 5 status buckets even when zero count (good for UI rendering).
/admin/orders search uses re.escape(qs) on regex — SAFE against ReDoS / regex injection. Limit clamped 1..500. $or across 6 fields covers all reasonable search axes.

/admin/orders/{id} PATCH validates status against VALID_ORDER_STATUS enum, rejects empty body with 400, returns 404 for unknown order, mirrors driver_id to linked trip when present. All edge cases handled.
MongoDB _id properly stripped from all admin responses via .pop('_id', None).

Frontend AdminDashboard.jsx (440 lines) is large but single-responsibility (PinScreen, Dashboard, StatTile, OrderCard, ConvertModal). data-testid coverage is excellent — every interactive element has a stable selector.
PIN cache via localStorage with re-auth check on mount (line 33-42): on cached PIN, fetches /admin/auth to validate before showing dashboard. Clean — handles PIN revocation by admin.
Logout (line 55) properly clears localStorage + resets PIN state + reverts to PinScreen.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_admin_v26d.py (NEW — 24 pytest covering: auth 3, guard 4, stats 1, list/filter/search 8, patch 6, regression 2)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (PIN gate, status workflow, search/filter, convert modal, driver mirror, logout, mobile responsive — all PASS)

test_credentials: ADMIN_PIN=0000 (set in /app/backend/.env). PIN screen accepts '0000', rejects all other values with 401.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned-up ~5 TEST_ prefixed orders (TEST_AdminBob/Cindy/David/Convert/E2E_AdminFlow). All set to CANCELLED at teardown to avoid polluting NEW count. Mongo cleanup query: db.orders.deleteMany({customer_nama: /^TEST_/}); db.trips.deleteMany({trip_id: /^TRIP-ORD-/, customer_data: {$exists: true}}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: v2.6d is STABLE. Adds Admin Mini-Dashboard at /?admin=1, PIN-gated by ADMIN_PIN env (default '0000'). Backend endpoints: POST /api/admin/auth (body PIN), GET /api/admin/stats, GET /api/admin/orders?status=&q=&limit=, PATCH /api/admin/orders/{id}. All require X-Admin-Pin header except /admin/auth. Frontend caches PIN in localStorage key 'aal_admin_pin'. Status workflow: NEW→DISPATCHED (via /orders/{id}/convert)→ON_TRIP→DELIVERED, CANCELLED terminal. Convert modal writes driver_id to TRIP only — order.driver_id requires inline edit after convert (see action item). 130/130 backend tests pass. No regressions from v2.6c.
rca of the issue: No critical issues. Single data-consistency nit: POST /api/orders/{id}/convert with driver_id in payload populates trip.driver_id but leaves order.driver_id null. Admin workaround = inline ✎ edit on the order card (PATCH /admin/orders/{id}) which correctly mirrors driver_id to BOTH order and trip. Either: (a) update convert endpoint to also write driver_id to order document, or (b) document that admin should always assign driver via inline edit (current UX path works fine since modal driver field is optional).
